该投资将用于扩展美国的沉浸式发行，并利用这么多人在大流行后工作引起的网络威胁的新兴趣。沉浸式实验室公司成立于2017年，现在有200人，在布里斯托尔，英国和美国波士顿的联合运营HQS。它计划在未来两年内向超过600升至600多个，并在整个APAC和欧洲建立新地区的业务。沉浸式的“网络劳动力优化”平台声称提供了董事会级指标和基准测试，以衡量组织内部技能如何应对。
 沉浸式现在已筹集了12300万美元的风险资金，并计入汇丰银行，沃达丰和NHS作为客户。该公司表示，它正在增长“同比超过100％”。
  詹姆斯·哈德利，沉浸式实验室的首席执行官和创始人表示：“随着网络风险越来越多的业务功能，网络安全知识和技能不再是在后台隐藏的一些技术人员的保存。来自CEO的团队中的团队中的每个人都需要在解决普遍的公司问题时扮演他们的一部分。这需要在更广泛的人群中解锁和证明技能。“
 洞察力伙伴董事总经理Ryan Hinkle表示：“随着过去几年的重大全球客户和收入增长，沉浸式实验室在快速发展的网络技能空间中建立了强有力的地位。随着有影响力的领导，一个创新的产品，在不断增长的市场中，以及强大的用户订婚，该公司能够继续领导网络准备市场。“
 在接受采访时对我说话：“我们选择了Insight Partners，因为他们在Enterprise B2B中获得了真正的力量，我们销售给CIO和CEOS ......我们希望成为一个成功的英国网络安全方面的下一个暗症星公司。”
 比较可能不是那种幻想。沉浸式实验室从类似于Darktrace的Cycon Cyber​​ Accelerator中出来，与Darktrace相同的投资者，但实际上它已经吸引了75米，而其系列的C型，而Darktrace没有管理那个级别，直到D. Darktrace现在已经Ipo' D在伦敦1.7亿英镑。
 前GCHQ安全研究员和培训师Hadley提出了网络技能平台的想法，同时领先网络训练自己。我问他为什么认为沉浸导致它的平台上有一个“飞轮效应”。 
“人们总是谈论所有的网络威胁都变得更糟，但它真的是现在和它在公共领域。 我们非常相信网络安全不再是地下缘怪人的责任。 实际上，它是商业范围的。 现在潮汐浪潮即将来临。 网络犯罪将在今年的比例下，下一步是因为公司正在支付赎金。 因此，我们正在进行分析来衡量危机的决策。 无论CIO或垂直如何，它只是对每家公司的共鸣非常好，“他告诉我。
note_en:
The investment will be used to scale Immersive’s offering in the US and take advantage of the new wave of interest in cyber threats caused by so many people working remotely, post-pandemic. Founded in 2017, Immersive Labs now has 200 people, with joint operations HQs in Bristol, UK, and Boston, US. It plans to raise headcount to over 600 in the next two years and establish operations in new regions throughout APAC and Europe. Immersive’s ‘Cyber Workforce Optimization’ platform claims to offer board-level metrics and benchmarking to gauge how the skills inside organizations are coping.
 Immersive has now raised a total of $123m in venture funding and counts HSBC, Vodafone, and the NHS as customers. The company says it is growing at “over 100% year-on-year”.
  James Hadley, CEO and founder of Immersive Labs, said: “With cyber risk becoming a problem for a growing number of business functions, cybersecurity knowledge and skills should no longer be the preserve of a few technical people hidden away in a back office. Everyone from the teams who build software, to the CEO, now need to play their part in addressing a pervasive company issue. This requires unlocking and evidencing skills in a much broader group of people.”
 Ryan Hinkle, managing director at Insight Partners, said: “With significant global customer and revenue growth over the last few years, Immersive Labs has established a strong position in the fast-developing cyber skills space. With influential leadership, an innovative product in a growing market, and strong user engagement, the company is in a position to continue to lead the cyber readiness market.”
 Speaking to me over an interview, Hadley added: “We chose Insight Partners because they’ve got a real strength in enterprise B2B which is where we sell to CIOs and CEOs… We want to be the next Darktrace in terms of a successful UK cybersecurity company.”
 The comparison might not be that fanciful. Immersive Labs came out of the CYLON cyber accelerator, similar to Darktrace, has the same investors as Darktrace, but has in fact attracted $75m for its Series C, whereas Darktrace didn’t manage that level until Series D. Darktrace has now IPO’d in the London for £1.7bn.
 Hadley, a former GCHQ security researcher and trainer, came up with the idea for the cyber skills platform while leading cyber training himself. I asked him why he thinks Immersive has managed to come up with a ‘flywheel effect’ with its platform.
 “People always talk about all the cyber threats getting worse, but it really is now and it’s in the public domain. We’ve got a strong belief that cybersecurity is no longer the responsibility of the geeks in the basement. Actually, it’s business-wide. And now the tidal wave is coming. Cybercrime is going to go off the scale this year and next because companies are paying the ransoms. And as a result of that, we’re putting in analytics to measure decision-making in a crisis. It’s just resonating really well with every company regardless of CIO or vertical,” he told me.
title: 网络安全培训平台沉浸式实验室闭贴了7500万美元的洞察力合作伙伴
title_en: Cyber security training platform Immersive Labs  closes $75M Series C led by Insight Partners

   商业视频游戏通常由专有的封闭源软件产品开发，源代码作为商业秘密（与开源视频游戏不同）。当没有更多的预期收入时，这些游戏将作为一个没有支持或可用性的产品进入寿命终结和排放的用户和社区，变得被遗弃。
  在这里列出的几个案例中，游戏和＃39; S开发人员明确发布了源代码，以防止他们的工作成为加法器。此类源代码通常在不同的（免费和非自由，商业和非商业和非商业）软件许可证下发布到游戏＆＃39;社区或公众;艺术品和数据通常在不同的许可下释放而不是源代码，因为版权情况是不同或更复杂的。源代码可以由开发人员推向公共存储库（例如sourceforge或github），或者给予所选的游戏社区成员，或者用游戏销售，或者通过其他方式获得。游戏可以用解释的语言编写，例如基本或Python，并且在没有被编译的情况下分发为原始源代码;早期软件通常以文本形式分发，如书籍基本电脑游戏。在某些情况下，游戏＆＃39; s源代码不可用其他方式，游戏＆＃39; s社区＆＃34;重建＆＃34;通过时间苛刻的逆向工程技术来自编译二进制文件的源代码。任何形式的源代码可用性允许游戏＆＃39;例如，当官方支持结束时，历史如何研究游戏工作，修改和提供技术支持，并为自己提供技术支持。使用非官方修补程序来修复错误或源端口，使游戏与新平台兼容。

Jump to navigation  Jump to search  This is a  list of commercial video games with  available source code. The  source code of these commercially developed and distributed  video games is available to the public or the games' communities.
   Commercial video games are typically developed as  proprietary  closed source  software products, with the source code treated as a  trade secret (unlike  open-source video games). When there is no more expected revenue, these games enter the  end-of-life as a product with no support or availability for the game's users and community, becoming  abandoned.
  In several of the cases listed here, the game's developers released the source code expressly to prevent their work from becoming abandonware. Such source code is often released under varying (free and non-free, commercial and non-commercial)  software licenses to the games' communities or the public; artwork and data are often released under a different license than the source code, as the copyright situation is different or more complicated. The source code may be pushed by the developers to public repositories (e.g.  SourceForge or  GitHub), or given to selected game community members, or sold with the game, or become available by other means. The game may be written in an  interpreted language such as  BASIC or  Python, and distributed as raw source code without being  compiled; early software was often distributed in text form, as in the book   BASIC Computer Games. In some cases when a game's source code is not available by other means, the game's community "reconstructs" source code from compiled  binary files through time-demanding  reverse engineering techniques. Source code availability in whatever form allows the games' communities to study how the game works, make modifications, and provide technical support themselves when the official support has ended, e.g. with  unofficial patches to fix bugs or  source ports to make the game compatible with new  platforms.
  In July 2013 the  source code of the game was put on  GitHub under  MPL 2.0.  [1] Became commercially successful after the source code release.
 Richard Garriott distributed the  Applesoft BASIC written game originally as source code. Also later  Origin Systems offered the source code on their  FTP servers.  [2]  [3]
 Source code released to the public under no license on June 11, 2021 upon the cancellation of the game.  [4]
